mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, reducing present value of S&P 500 earnings and producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42% as of 2018-02-01 in early tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rising FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives consumption and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum over 60-day horizon falls below 0.02 in any 2-year rolling window, or positive employment growth periods do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative growth periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,326 thousand (vintage) vs 1,299 thousand (revised) as of 2018-01-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST below-MA signal remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative HOUST periods do not produce significantly lower forward returns than positive periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, which signals robust corporate profitability and capital investment. Rising INDPRO produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO growth signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or high-growth INDPRO periods do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than low-growth periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and real consumer spending growth jointly signal household purchasing power and demand strength. When both RSAFS and PAYEMS accelerate simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,008 million (vintage) vs 484,031 million (revised) as of 2018-02-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint RSAFS-PAYEMS acceleration signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or co-accelerating periods do not produce significantly higher forward returns than single-series acceleration (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate aggressive future rate hikes.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of inflation-rate-lag signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods meeting both trigger conditions do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk. This threshold triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand in response to employment weakness.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE > 4.5% threshold signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods with UNRATE > 4.5% do not produce significantly lower forward returns than UNRATE <= 4.5% periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 combined with housing starts HOUST declining simultaneously signals demand-side weakness despite headline growth, indicating profit margin compression. This divergence produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprrice earnings quality.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND HOUST 3-month average declining
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,271.702 billion (vintage) vs 19,882.352 billion (revised) as of 2017-10-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDP-housing divergence signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods with both conditions met do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than periods with only GDP growth (t-test p > 0.05).
